definition: Approximately $200-400B of BBB- rated debt is at risk of downgrade to high yield, where IG-mandated forced selling is mechanical and HY absorption capacity of ~$50-100B can trigger non-linear spread dislocation. This converts gradual credit deterioration into discrete correlation-flipping events.
evidence: Rating migration trends: upgrade/downgrade ratios deteriorating from ~1.5x to ~0.9-1.0x
Each historical instance coincided with negative correlation shift
HY market has limited capacity for sudden large inflows
open_questions: At-risk BBB- cohort size ranges widely ($200-400B)
Historical analogues have highly variable magnitudes
What specific issuers are most vulnerable to the fallen angel trigger?
